originalTitle: South Africa president faces call to resign after court ruling
title: A Big Rule-Check in South Africa!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends all over Africa! It's your friendly kid-reporter here, bringing you super important news from our amazing continent. Today, we're talking about a very special country called South Africa and its president.

Meet President Cyril

Do you know what a president does? A president is like the head leader of a whole country! They work super hard to make sure everything runs smoothly, that people are safe, and that good rules are made to help everyone live happily. Think of them as the captain of a very big ship – the country! The president of South Africa is a kind man named Cyril Ramaphosa. He has a very important job, helping all the people in his country every single day. He makes big decisions and helps guide the nation. Did you know? South Africa is famous for its beautiful wildlife, like lions, elephants, and rhinos! It’s like a giant outdoor classroom for nature lovers.

A Special Country: South Africa

South Africa is a beautiful country right at the very bottom tip of our continent, Africa. It has sunny beaches, tall mountains, and bustling cities. Many different people live there, speaking many different languages, which makes it a very colourful and exciting place! Just like in our schools, where we have rules to keep things fair and safe, countries also have very important rules. These are called laws. Laws help everyone know what is right and wrong, and they make sure that everyone, from the youngest child to the oldest person, follows the same standards.

Rules, Rules, Rules!

Why do we have rules? Imagine playing a game without any rules – it would be messy and unfair, right? That’s why rules are so important in a country too. They help everyone know how to behave and ensure that things are fair for everybody. Even the president has to follow the rules! In South Africa, like in many countries, there's a special book of very important rules called the Constitution. This book holds the most important rules for the whole country, like how people should be treated and how leaders should do their jobs. It’s like the rulebook for the whole country.

What is a Court?

Now, sometimes, people might not agree on how the rules should be followed, or maybe someone thinks a rule was broken. When this happens, they go to a special place called a court. A court is where wise judges listen to everyone and decide what is fair and what the rules really mean. Did you know? There are different kinds of courts! Some courts help with small problems, and then there are very big, important courts. In South Africa, there's a very special court called the Constitutional Court. It’s the highest court, and its job is to make sure that everyone, even the government and the president, follows the big rules in the Constitution. It's like the head referee for the country's biggest rulebook!

The Big Check-Up: Impeachment

Now for a bit of a tricky word: 'impeachment'. Don't worry, it's not as scary as it sounds! Impeachment is simply a very important process where special people in the government check very, very carefully if a very important leader, like the president, has always followed all the big rules. It’s like a big, thorough check-up to make sure everything is okay. If these people find that something went wrong, and the rules were not followed properly, they might then decide if that leader should continue in their important job or not. It’s a way to keep leaders honest and accountable to the rules. Did you know? In many countries, there is a special building where elected people meet to make rules. This building is often called 'Parliament'. In South Africa, the Parliament is where many Members of Parliament, or 'MPs', work.

What Happened with the Court?

Recently, the special Constitutional Court in South Africa looked at something that happened. A group of important people called 'MPs' (Members of Parliament) had stopped the big 'impeachment' check-up for President Cyril Ramaphosa. They had decided not to go ahead with looking into whether any rules were broken. But the Constitutional Court, the super-important rule-checker, said: "Wait a minute!" The judges looked at what the MPs did and said that it was wrong for them to stop the check-up. The court said that the MPs *should* have continued to check very carefully, to make sure everything was fair and that all rules were followed. It was like the court telling them, "You need to finish your homework and check all your answers!" So, the court is saying that the MPs need to go back and finish the job of carefully checking to see if all the rules were followed by the President. This is a very big deal because it shows that even the people who make rules must follow the rules about checking leaders.

Why This Matters

Why is this news important for all of us, even kids? It's important because it shows that *everyone* in a country, no matter how important their job is, must follow the rules. It shows that courts are very important for making sure things are fair and that leaders are honest. It means that the rules are for everybody! When a court makes a decision like this, it reminds everyone about 'accountability'. That's another big word, but it just means being responsible for your actions and being able to explain what you did. It's like when your teacher asks you why you didn't do your homework; you have to be accountable for it.

What Happens Next?

Now, because of what the court said, the MPs in South Africa will have to go back and continue the check-up process for President Ramaphosa. They will have to look into things very carefully, just as the court said. Some people are even saying that the president should 'resign', which means leave his job, but that's a big decision and something that will be decided after all the careful checking is done. It’s a powerful message that the rulebook for the country is very important and must be respected by everyone, including the highest leaders. It shows that justice and fairness are really important in South Africa, and all over Africa. So, my friends, that's the big news from South Africa! Remember, following rules keeps things fair and happy for everyone. Stay curious, stay kind, and keep learning about our amazing continent!
summary: South Africa's highest court has told important rule-makers (MPs) that they must go back and properly check if President Cyril Ramaphosa followed all the country's big rules, making sure everyone, even leaders, are fair.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that everyone, even the president, must follow the rules of the country, and special courts are there to make sure everything is fair and honest.

originalTitle: 'I'd rather live in hiding in the US than return to Somalia'
title: Our Friends from Somalia: Looking for a Safe Home Far Away
content:
Hello, Wonderful Friends!

Hello everyone! It’s your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you a very important story from far away. We are going to talk about some special friends who are looking for a safe place to live. It's about people from a country called Somalia. They are trying to find a place where they can feel safe and happy every day. Sometimes, bad things happen in a place that make people feel scared. When this happens, people sometimes have to leave their homes to find a new, safe home.

Where is Somalia?

Somalia is a beautiful country in Africa. It is on the eastern side, right next to the big blue ocean. It has lots of sunshine and lovely people. But sometimes, for a long time, there have been big problems in Somalia. These problems can make people feel very unsafe and worried. **Did you know?** Somalia is famous for its long coastline! That means it has lots and lots of beaches next to the ocean. Isn't that cool? When things are not safe, it’s hard to go to school, or play outside, or even just feel calm at home. So, some families decide they must go somewhere else. They hope to find a new place where children can play freely and everyone can sleep without worry. It's a very big and brave journey to make.

Why Do People Go on Big Journeys?

Imagine if your street was not safe. Imagine if it was hard to find food sometimes, or if there were loud noises that made you jump. That's how it can feel for some people in places where there is trouble. They might not have safe water or good hospitals. So, they pack their bags and go on a very long journey. They go to a new place, hoping for a better life. This is called moving, or being a "migrant." It's like when a little bird flies south for the winter to find warm weather. People do it too, to find safety and happiness. **Did you know?** People have been moving to new places all over the world for thousands of years! It's a part of our big human story. They travel very, very far away. Sometimes they go to another country that is very different from their own home.

Living Far Away in America

Many people from Somalia have traveled all the way to a country called America. It is very, very far across the ocean. One place they live in America is called Minnesota. It’s a very cold place in America sometimes, with lots of snow! Very different from sunny Somalia. Even though they are far away and in a new country, some of our Somali friends still feel a little bit scared. They have new homes and new schools, but deep inside, they still worry about what will happen to them next. They worry if they will be able to stay in this new safe place, or if they might have to go back to Somalia where it felt unsafe.

A Little Bit of Good News!

But guess what? We have some good news for them! Recently, the people who make big rules in America said something important. They said that many of these Somali friends can stay in America for a little while longer. It’s like getting a special permission slip. This means they won't have to leave right away. They have a chance to show why they need to stay safe in America. This special permission is called a "legal reprieve." It’s a big, fancy word that just means they get a bit more time to be safe. It's like when you are playing a game, and you get an extra turn or a chance to try again. It gives them hope!

But Still a Little Scared

Even with this good news, some of our friends still feel a little bit scared. They are happy for the extra time. But they know it's not forever. They still worry about what will happen after this special time is over. They remember how hard it was in Somalia. They don't want to go back if it's not safe for them and their families. Imagine always thinking about "what if." What if I have to leave my new friends? What if I have to leave my new school? It makes their hearts feel a bit fluttery, like a butterfly that isn't sure where to land next. This is called "uncertainty." **Did you know?** Feeling scared or worried about something that might happen is a normal feeling! Everyone feels it sometimes.

What is "Hiding"?

The story says that some people would rather "live in hiding" than go back. That sounds a bit mysterious, doesn't it? Living in hiding means you can't live like everyone else. You might not be able to go to school or to the doctor easily. You have to be very careful. You might not want many people to know where you live or who you are. It means not having papers that say you are allowed to be in a place. It means always worrying about being found out. This is a very hard way to live. Children want to go to school and play. Grown-ups want to work and help their families. **Did you know?** Every child in the world has a right to feel safe, to go to school, and to have a home! They don't want to hide. They just want a place where they can be themselves, without fear, and build a good life.

Everyone Wants to Be Safe

This story reminds us that everyone, no matter where they come from, wants to feel safe. They want a home where they are loved. They want to be able to go to school, to play with friends, and to learn new things. Just like you do! Our Somali friends in America are still hoping for a forever home where they can feel truly safe and happy. They are brave for sharing their story. They remind us to think about people who are far away, and what they need. Let's all remember that kindness is a superpower. We can send good thoughts to them and hope they find their permanent safe place soon.

Let's Be Kind

Remember, it’s important to be kind to everyone. Especially to people who have traveled far to find safety. We can imagine how brave they are. And we can hope that they find a home where their hearts can feel peaceful and happy. Let's all wish our Somali friends the very best! They deserve a safe and happy home, just like everyone else.
summary: People from Somalia who moved to America for safety are happy they can stay longer, but they still worry about finding a permanent safe home.
whyMatters: This story is important because it shows that everyone needs a safe place to live, and it helps us understand why people sometimes have to leave their homes to find peace.

originalTitle: Ethiopian woman's joy at rare quintuplets after 12 years trying for a baby
title: Ethiopian woman's joy at rare quintuplets after 12 years trying for a baby
content: The woman, 35, says she was praying for a baby and was "overjoyed" to be "blessed with five at once".

originalTitle: Laura Fernandez sworn in as Costa Rica’s new president
title: Big News from Far Away: Costa Rica Gets a New Boss!
content:
Hello from Your Friendly News Reporter!

Hello, my wonderful friends all across Africa! It’s your friendly news reporter here, ready to share some super exciting news from a faraway land! Today, we’re going to talk about a country called Costa Rica. It’s not in Africa, it’s actually very far across the big, big ocean, but it's still interesting to learn about other places! Something really important just happened there. A wonderful lady named Laura Fernandez became the new boss of their whole country! Isn't that something?

Meet the New Boss: Laura Fernandez!

Imagine your school. You have a principal, right? Someone who helps everyone, makes important decisions, and tries to make the school a great place for learning and playing. Well, a country also needs a special person like that. We call this person a 'President'. The President is like the main leader, the big boss, of a whole country! They are in charge of helping everyone. And guess what? In Costa Rica, a lady named Laura Fernandez has just become their new President! Isn't that amazing? She is a very important person now. She is now in charge of helping her country grow, making good rules, and looking after all the people who live there, just like a principal looks after a school. She has a big job ahead!

Where is Costa Rica?

Now, you might be wondering, where exactly is this Costa Rica place? Is it near your home? No, my friends, it's quite a trip! Costa Rica is a beautiful country located far away in a place called Central America. It's like a bridge connecting North America and South America, right between two big oceans. It's famous for many amazing things, like its incredible rainforests, beautiful beaches, and lots and lots of wonderful animals! Did you know? Costa Rica has lots of colourful birds, like toucans, cheeky monkeys swinging through trees, sleepy sloths moving slowly, and even cool frogs with bright colours! It's like a giant natural playground filled with nature. It's also a very peaceful country. They don't even have an army! Instead, they use their money to help their people, like building schools and hospitals and protecting their nature. Isn't that a lovely idea?

What Does "Sworn In" Mean?

So, our news says that Laura Fernandez was 'sworn in' as President. That sounds a bit grown-up, right? Let me explain! When someone is 'sworn in', it means they make a very important promise. It's like when you promise your friend you'll share your toys, or when you promise your parents you'll help with chores. It's a serious promise. But for a President, this promise is super big! They promise to do their very best for their country, to follow the rules, and to be fair to everyone. They promise to work hard for all the people. It's a special ceremony where they stand up, often with their hand on a special book, and say out loud that they will do their job well and with all their heart. It's a moment when everyone knows they are taking on this big responsibility. This promise shows everyone that they are ready to start their important work as the leader of the country. It's a big step!

Working Together: The President's Team!

Now, being a President is a very big job, and one person cannot do it all by themselves. They need a team to help them! Imagine playing a football game. You need a captain, but you also need all the other players to win, right? It's the same for a country. The President needs people to work with. Our news also says something about 'her right-wing party holding an absolute majority in the legislature'. Wow, those are some big words! Let's break it down simply. A 'party' in this grown-up world is like a group of friends who all have similar ideas about how to make the country better. They all believe in the same plans and goals. It's like having different groups in your class who have ideas for the school play – maybe one group wants a superhero play, and another wants an animal play. They each have their own ideas. Laura Fernandez belongs to one of these groups, and her group has lots of people who also won elections. This is good because it means many people agree with her ideas and will help her. Her team is strong!

Making Rules for Everyone: The Legislature

So, what about the 'legislature'? That's another big word, but it's actually very simple! The 'legislature' is like a big meeting place where people from different groups come together to talk and make rules for the country. We call these rules 'laws'. These laws help everyone live safely and fairly. Think of it like the school council, where students and teachers meet to decide things like what new books to get for the library or how to make the playground better. Our news says Laura Fernandez's group has an 'absolute majority' in this legislature. This means her group has more than half of the people in that big meeting place. They have more votes than all the other groups put together! Did you know? Having an 'absolute majority' is a bit like having more than half of the votes when you are deciding what game to play. If your team has more than half the votes, it's easier to make decisions and get things done quickly! It means they can often get their ideas turned into rules. This means it will be easier for Laura Fernandez and her team to make the changes and rules they promised to the people of Costa Rica. They can work together more smoothly!

Why Is This Important For Us, Even Far Away?

So, why should we care about what happens in Costa Rica, so far from Africa? Well, my friends, it's important because it shows us how people in different parts of the world choose their leaders and work together to make their countries better. Every country, big or small, needs good leaders who care about their people, just like every school needs a good principal and good teachers. Good leadership helps everyone. It teaches us about teamwork, making promises, and working towards a happy and safe future for everyone. It reminds us that even kids like you can grow up to be important leaders, helping your own communities and countries one day! So, let's wish President Laura Fernandez and the people of Costa Rica all the best in their journey!

originalTitle: Venice Biennale: The Art of the Unseen
title: Big Art Fun in a Water City: What's There and What's Not!
content:
Hello, hello, my wonderful friends all over Africa! It's your friendly news reporter, here to share a super interesting story from far, far away. Grab your comfy spot, because we're going on an adventure with our minds today! We're going to talk about art and big ideas, in a way that’s easy to understand, just for you! Are you ready? Let’s go!

Hello from a Faraway Water City!

Imagine a city where the roads are not for cars, but for boats! Yes, really! This super special city is called Venice. It's in a country far away across the sea, called Italy. People there travel on water roads, which we call canals. They use special, pretty boats called gondolas. It’s like magic! Instead of walking or driving on solid ground, you float everywhere. It's very different from our homes here in Africa, isn't it? Did you know? Venice has no cars or motorbikes! Everyone walks or travels by boat. Isn't that a fun fact? The buildings in Venice are very old and beautiful. They stand right by the water, looking like they've been there for hundreds and hundreds of years. The sunlight makes the water sparkle, and the boats gently rock. It’s a very famous and special place on our big, blue Earth. Many people dream of visiting Venice one day.

What is the Big Art Party?

Now, in this amazing water city, something very exciting happens every two years. It's like a gigantic art party! It’s called the Venice Biennale. Can you say 'Bee-enn-ah-leh'? It means 'every two years'. So, every two years, artists from all over the world come to Venice. They bring their most wonderful and surprising art to show everyone. Imagine walking into a giant room and seeing a painting as big as a house! Or a sculpture that looks like a friendly monster! That's what happens at the Biennale. What is art, you ask? Art can be anything! It can be a painting with bright colors, a statue made of wood or metal, or even a photograph that tells a story. Art makes us feel things and think new thoughts. It’s like a special language that everyone can understand, no matter where they live. People from countries near and far, even from different parts of Africa, share their art. They want to show what they imagine and what they believe is important. Did you know? Art has been around for thousands of years! Even our great-great-grandparents made art. In Africa, we have so much beautiful art – from colourful fabrics to amazing masks and carvings. Art is part of who we are and tells stories about our history and our dreams for the future. So, this big art party in Venice is a bit like a giant storybook where artists show their special stories.

Seeing Art, and Not Seeing Art

Now, this year, something very interesting happened at the big art party in Venice. It wasn't just about the art that everyone could see. It was also about the art that was... well, *missing*! Yes, you heard me right. Some art, or even some artists and countries, were not there. This might sound a little bit strange, right? Why would people go to a big art party and then not show their art? Sometimes, people decide not to show their art because they want to send a special message. It's like when you stay quiet to show someone you're serious, or you don't play a game to show you don't agree with a rule. These artists and countries decided to be quiet with their art. They wanted everyone to think about *why* they were quiet, and *why* their art was not there. It makes people think harder about the world.

What You Can See!

Even with some art missing, there was still so much to see! Imagine rooms filled with colours that jump off the walls. Sculptures that look like giants or tiny creatures. Artists use all sorts of materials – paint, wood, metal, plastic, and even things from nature like leaves and stones. It's like a giant treasure hunt for your eyes! Every corner you turn, there's a new surprise waiting for you. Artists from many different places shared their work. They showed how different people see the world. Some art was happy and bright, some was calm and peaceful, and some made you feel like you wanted to run and play! It’s all about sharing feelings and ideas through shapes and colors. You can learn so much about other countries and other people just by looking at their art. It’s a wonderful way to travel the world without leaving the room! Did you know? Some artists paint with their fingers! And some make art so big, you can walk inside it! The world of art is full of endless possibilities. It’s all about using your imagination and showing everyone what you dream about. Just like you can draw a picture of your family or your favourite animal, artists in Venice show their big dreams to the world.

The Art of Not Being Seen

But let's go back to the idea of the missing art. This is what makes this art party very special this year. When some artists decide not to show their work, it means they want people to *think* about what’s *not* there. It's like if you expect to see a beautiful flower in a garden, but then you see an empty spot. You would wonder, 'Why isn't there a flower here?' And that question makes you think. This 'unseen art' or 'missing art' is still very powerful. It tells a story too, just a different kind of story. It makes people ask big questions about fairness, and about who gets to share their voice. It’s like when we listen carefully for a sound, and if it’s not there, that absence itself can tell us something important. It’s a quiet way of making a very loud point. It really gets people talking and thinking. Did you know? Sometimes, the most important messages are not shouted, but whispered or even shown by staying silent. In art, being quiet can be a way to make people really stop and pay attention. It shows that artists care deeply about important things in the world, and they want everyone else to care too. It’s a very grown-up way to use art to make a difference.

Why is this Important for Us, Even in Africa?

You might be thinking, 'Why is this big art party in far-away Venice important for us, here in Africa?' Well, art connects all of us, no matter where we live. When artists make choices about showing or not showing their art, it helps everyone around the world think about important things. It helps us think about different countries, different people, and different ways of seeing the world. It also teaches us that our voices matter. Even when we are small, we can make choices that help others think. If you draw a picture to show how you feel, or if you choose not to draw something because you want to make a point, that is important. Art helps us understand each other better. It helps us learn about peace, about fairness, and about being kind to everyone. Did you know? Africa has so many amazing artists who create wonderful pieces that tell our stories and show our beautiful cultures! From ancient rock art to modern paintings and sculptures, African art is celebrated all over the world. We too use art to share our feelings, our history, and our hopes. So, this story from Venice is really about all of us, and how we share our world.

Let's Make Our Own Art!

So, my dear friends, next time you draw a picture or make something with your hands, remember the artists in Venice. Think about what you want to show with your art. Do you want to show something beautiful? Do you want to tell a story? Or do you want to make people think about something important by showing something, or even by *not* showing something? Every piece of art you make is special. It's your way of sharing your ideas with the world. Keep exploring, keep imagining, and keep creating! You are all amazing artists in your own right. And remember, every story, every picture, every song, helps us understand our big, wide world a little bit better. That’s all from your African news friend today! Until next time, keep smiling, keep learning, and keep being kind!
summary: At a big art show in Venice, artists from around the world share their work, but this year, some art is missing, which makes people think about important ideas and messages that can be powerful even when not seen.
whyMatters: This is important because art helps us understand different people and ideas, and sometimes, even when art is not shown, it can send a very strong message that makes everyone think about fairness and important things in the world, connecting us all, even here in Africa.

originalTitle: Could Labour and Conservative party dominance in UK politics be ending?
title: Could Labour and Conservative party dominance in UK politics be ending?
content: Britain's prime minister is under pressure to quit after huge losses in local elections.

originalTitle: Iran accuses US of 'reckless military adventure'
title: Let's Talk Peace, Not Fights! What Countries Are Saying!
content:
Hello my amazing friends, future leaders, and wonderful explorers across our beautiful continent of Africa! Your friendly news reporter is here to share some important happenings from around our big, wide world today. I hope you're ready to learn about how countries, just like people, sometimes have disagreements, and how they try to solve them. Today, we're going to talk about something very important: how countries get along... or sometimes, how they don't! It's a bit like when you and your friend have a disagreement over a toy or a game. Do you shout and push, or do you sit down and talk it out? Most of the time, talking is the best way, right?

Who Said What?

Imagine two very big countries far, far away. One is called Iran, which is a country in Asia with lots of ancient history and beautiful art. The other is called the United States of America, or USA for short. The USA is a very big country in North America, known for its tall buildings and diverse people. Someone very important from Iran, his name is Mr. Abbas Araghchi, said something about the USA. He is like a special messenger for his country, a 'diplomat' who helps Iran talk to other countries. He said that sometimes, when people from different countries are trying really hard to talk and find a peaceful way to solve their problems, the USA does things that are not very helpful. He thinks these actions make it harder for everyone to be friends and find solutions. He called these actions a 'reckless military adventure'. That's a big phrase, isn't it? Let's break it down so we can understand it better, like solving a fun puzzle!

What Does 'Accuses' Mean?

When someone 'accuses' another person or country, it means they are saying that person or country did something wrong. It's like if your friend says, "Hey, you ate the last cookie!" They are accusing you of eating the cookie. It doesn't mean it's definitely true, but it means someone is pointing a finger and saying, "You did this!" So, Mr. Araghchi from Iran is saying the USA is doing things that make it harder to have peace. He is sharing Iran's point of view. It's not a fight with fists, but with words, about actions that one country feels are not fair or helpful. It's important to listen to both sides of a story, always.

'Reckless Military Adventure' - What's That?

Let's understand this big phrase. 'Reckless' means doing something without thinking carefully about the dangers or what might happen next. Imagine running super fast without looking where you're going – that could be a bit reckless, right? You might trip and hurt yourself or someone else! 'Military' means anything to do with soldiers, armies, and sometimes, their special equipment like planes, ships, or tanks. These are things used to protect a country, but they can also be used in conflicts. 'Adventure' usually means something exciting and fun, like exploring a new place! But here, it's used in a way that means doing something risky or dangerous, especially with the military. So, Iran is saying the USA sometimes acts without thinking carefully, using its army, when everyone should be talking peacefully to find solutions. Mr. Araghchi feels these actions are not smart and could cause more problems instead of solving them. **Did You Know?** The word 'reckless' comes from an old English word meaning 'careless'. So, it means acting without enough care!

Talking Things Out: The 'Diplomatic Solution'

Mr. Araghchi also talked about 'diplomatic solutions'. This is a super important idea for everyone, everywhere! A 'diplomatic solution' means finding a peaceful way to solve problems between countries. It's like when you and your friend have a fight over a toy, and instead of pushing or shouting, you both sit down with a grown-up and talk about sharing, or taking turns, or finding another toy to play with. That's a small-scale diplomatic solution! Leaders from different countries meet, they talk, they listen very carefully to each other, and they try to find a way for everyone to be happy, or at least to stop arguing. They might make promises or sign agreements on paper to keep the peace and be fair to each other. **Did You Know?** People who help countries talk to each other are called 'diplomats'. They are very good at listening and finding fair ways to solve problems so that everyone can live peacefully. It's a very important job!

Why Is Talking So Important?

Talking is super powerful! When people or countries talk, they can understand each other better. They can explain why they are upset, what they need, or what they are worried about. It's like when you tell your mum or dad what's bothering you, and they can help you feel better. Imagine if nobody ever talked! It would be very hard to make friends, to learn, or to solve any problems, wouldn't it? Talking helps to prevent small problems from growing into much bigger, scarier problems, like a tiny spark turning into a huge bushfire. It helps everyone live together more peacefully on our amazing planet Earth. When countries talk, they can share ideas, trade goods, and even help each other when there are big challenges like a flood or an illness. **Did You Know?** There's a special building in New York, USA, called the United Nations. It's a place where almost all countries in the world can send their diplomats to talk and try to solve big problems together! It's like a big meeting place for the whole world!

What Iran Said Was Happening

So, what Mr. Araghchi from Iran is saying is that every time countries are *this close* to finding a diplomatic solution, to solving problems with words and kindness, the USA sometimes does something with its military. He feels like these actions make it harder for the talking to work and for peace to truly happen. It's like when you're almost finished building a tall block tower with a friend, and then someone bumps the table and makes it wobble or fall down. Oops! He thinks that these actions by the USA are not helping the peace talks and can make things more difficult for everyone.

Our Big, Wonderful World

Our world is full of different countries, with different languages, different foods, and different ways of doing things. Just like our own continent of Africa, with so many diverse countries and cultures! We learn to live and trade peacefully, mostly, and we help each other grow. Sometimes countries have disagreements, just like we sometimes disagree with our friends or siblings. But the best way, the smartest way, and the kindest way to solve these disagreements is always to talk, to listen, and to try to understand. We hope that leaders from all countries will always choose to talk and find peaceful ways, diplomatic solutions, to make our world a happier and safer place for everyone, especially for all of you wonderful children! Peace means everyone can learn, play, and grow without fear. **Did You Know?** The longest border between two countries without any military protection is between Canada and the USA. They have a very long history of talking things out and being friendly neighbours!

Sending Wishes for Peace

So, remember my friends, talking is always better than fighting. Whether it's in your school, in your home, in your village, or between big countries far away. Your words are powerful tools for peace. We hope that Iran and the USA, and all countries, will keep trying their best to talk and find those peaceful, diplomatic solutions. Your friendly reporter here in Africa sends you big smiles and wishes for a world full of peace, understanding, and kindness for everyone!
summary: An important person from Iran, Mr. Abbas Araghchi, said that the USA sometimes acts without thinking carefully with its army when countries are trying to talk and find peaceful solutions, making it harder for peace to happen.
whyMatters: This matters because when big countries disagree and don't talk peacefully, it can make the world feel less safe for everyone, and it's always better for countries to solve problems with words rather than with actions that could lead to bigger troubles.

originalTitle: Trump says Russia and Ukraine to observe three-day ceasefire
title: Trump says Russia and Ukraine to observe three-day ceasefire
content: It comes as the two countries accused each other of violating separate ceasefires to cover the celebrations of the Soviet Union's victory over Nazi Germany.

title: Worldwide race to trace passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
content: About 12 countries have so far been linked to the hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius, as the UN health agency confirms at least five cases.

originalTitle: Iraola top of Palace shortlist for new manager
title: Iraola top of Palace shortlist for new manager
content: Bournemouth manager Andoni Iraola heads Crystal Palace's shortlist of candidates to replace outgoing head coach Oliver Glasner next season.

originalTitle: How Southampton allegedly spied on Middlesbrough
title: Football Fun: Playing Fair is Super Important!
content:
Hello, Wonderful Friends!

Jambo, amazing friends! Your kid-friendly news reporter from Africa is here! I have a really interesting story to share with you today. It's all about a game we love so much – football! Football is a super fun game, isn't it? Two teams play against each other. Each team tries to kick a ball into the other team's net to score a goal. It's like a big, exciting dance with a ball! Millions of people all over the world love football. Maybe you play it at school, or with your friends in the field, right? It's a game that brings everyone together. Did you know? In some places, like America, football is called 'soccer'! But it's the same wonderful game.

Meet the Teams: Southampton and Middlesbrough

Our story today is about two big football teams. One team is called Southampton. The other team is called Middlesbrough. They are both from a country far away called England. These teams play in a very special competition. It's like a big league, and it's called the Championship. All the teams in this league are very good players. Southampton and Middlesbrough are both strong teams. They work very hard to win their games. They really want to be the best! Sometimes, teams become 'rivals'. This means they both try extra hard to beat each other. It's like when you play a fun game with your friend, and you both want to win! These two teams were playing some very important games. These special games are called 'play-offs'. Play-offs are like extra chances to win a big prize. If you win these games, your team can move up to an even bigger and better league!

What Does 'Spying' Mean in Football?

Now, here's where our story gets really interesting. The news says that one team, Southampton, might have done something a little tricky. They might have 'spied' on the other team, Middlesbrough. What does 'spying' mean? It doesn't mean like a secret agent in a movie, with cool gadgets and invisibility cloaks. No, not like that at all! In football, 'spying' means trying to find out secrets about the other team without permission. It means watching them very, very closely. Maybe seeing how they practice their moves. Or it could mean trying to find out their secret plans for the next game. Like knowing which player will kick the ball in a special way, or where they plan to pass it. The news said it wasn't even very difficult to do this 'spying'. This means someone from Southampton might have watched Middlesbrough's practice secretly. Or they might have looked at their game plans when they weren't supposed to. Did you know? Teams usually keep their practice sessions private. This is so they can surprise their opponents with new strategies in the game!

Why Would a Team 'Spy'?

Teams always want to win, right? Winning feels great! Everyone loves to win. But there's a right way to win and a wrong way to win. Maybe Southampton wanted to win so, so badly. They might have thought that knowing Middlesbrough's secrets would help them win the game more easily. But using secret information that you weren't supposed to have is not fair. It's a bit like knowing all the answers to a test before you even study for it! That wouldn't feel right, would it? Real champions work hard. They train every day. They play skillfully and fairly. They win because they are the best, not because they knew secrets.

Rules Are Super Important!

So, why is this 'spying' a big deal? Why is it in the news? It's because football has rules! Just like all games, football has very important rules. Rules are there to make sure everyone plays fairly. Imagine playing a game where one person doesn't follow the rules. It wouldn't be fun or fair for anyone else, would it? In football, rules help make sure the game is exciting and fair for all the players and all the fans. They make sure everyone has an equal chance. When a team tries to 'spy' on another team, it might not be fair. It could give them an advantage that they didn't earn through hard work. The rules of football say that teams should not try to trick other teams this way. Everyone should play honestly and respectfully. Did you know? Playing fair is often called 'sportsmanship'. It means being kind and respectful to everyone, even when you really want to win!

What Does 'Under Investigation' Mean?

Because of this 'spying' story, Southampton is now 'under investigation'. What does 'under investigation' mean? It means that grown-ups in charge are looking very carefully into what happened. They want to find out the truth. It's a bit like when something happens at school, and your teacher asks lots of questions. The teacher wants to understand what really happened, right? The people who run the football league are asking questions. They want to know if Southampton truly 'spied'. They want to find out if any rules were broken. It's very important to them that football stays fair for all the teams and players. Everyone, even big football teams, must follow the rules.

What Happens Next?

When an investigation happens, people work to gather information. They talk to different people who might know something. They look for clues. Then, after they have all the information, they will decide if rules were truly broken. If they find out that rules were broken, there might be a punishment. Sometimes, the punishment could be a fine. That means the team might have to pay some money. Or it could be something else that helps teach teams to always play fair. We will all have to wait and see what the investigators find out. It's like waiting for the end of a mystery book to find out who did it! Did you know? Many sports around the world have special rules to stop teams from getting unfair advantages. It's all about keeping the game honest and fun!

Playing Fair is Always the Best Way!

My dear friends, this story teaches us a very important lesson. Playing fair is always, always the best way to play any game. It doesn't matter if you are playing football, or jumping rope, or playing a board game with your family. Always follow the rules and play fairly. When everyone plays fair, the game is more fun for everyone. It shows that you are a good friend and a good player, too. Winning is a wonderful feeling, but winning fairly is an even better feeling! It makes you feel truly proud of yourself and your team. So, let's remember to always cheer for fair play! And let's remember that everyone deserves a fair chance to win. It's all about fun, respect, and enjoying the beautiful game of football! That's all for today from your friendly reporter! Keep playing fair and keep smiling! See you next time, my wonderful friends!
summary: Football team Southampton is being investigated for allegedly 'spying' on rival team Middlesbrough, which means they might have secretly watched their practices or plans, breaking fair play rules.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches us that rules are very important in sports and in life, making sure everyone plays fairly and honestly, because winning honestly feels best.

originalTitle: Man Utd's Fernandes wins Football Writers' men's award
title: Man Utd's Fernandes wins Football Writers' men's award
content: Manchester United captain Bruno Fernandes wins the Football Writers' Association's men's Footballer of the Year award.
